commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d42cdb9824de783f22cfa69a04da5d0b188e6269)
commands.review_summary:
The PR appends a resolved workspace hash to non-shared sandbox scope keys, propagates that key into Docker, SSH, browser, skills, and registry identity, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +192. Total +208 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main and v2026.6.10 derive non-shared sandbox identity without workspace identity, and the canonical issue gives a two-HOME same-agent Docker collision path. I did not run Docker in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the upgrade/docs handling are required before merge.

target.title: fix(sandbox): scope agent sandboxes by workspace
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Agent-scoped sandboxes currently key reuse only by agent/session identity. In multi-tenant deployments, the same agent id can be used across separate customer workspaces, so a non-shared sandbox may be reused with the wrong workspace/container identity. This scopes non-shared sandbox lifetime keys by resolved workspace path while preserving shared sandbox behavior.

## Summary
- include the resolved workspace path hash in non-shared sandbox scope keys
- preserve the workspace hash outside the readable slug truncation window for Docker, SSH, and sandbox skills paths
- pass the resolved scope key through Docker sandbox creation so container names, labels, and registry entries stay workspace-specific
- cover session, shared, agent, Docker backend, and SSH backend behavior with regression tests

## Evidence
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/docker-backend.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.test.ts` passed locally: 6 files, 62 tests
- `git diff --check` passed locally

## Notes
- Existing pre-PR non-shared sandbox containers use the old scope key. After this change, they will be recreated under workspace-scoped names and the old containers will age out through the existing sandbox prune path.
- Current broad `pnpm tsgo:core:test` rerun is blocked by unrelated/stale linked dependency failures (`vitest`, `typebox`, `lit`, `ws`, and existing UI/package type errors). Focused sandbox tests and diff checks pass.
target.author: tayoun

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
